query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "ace-ego-0: unifying egocentric human and robotic data for vla pretrain" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (b) by measuring the dependent variable of inference latency and computational cost (FLOPs) for Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, which is the core domain of the user's question regarding VLA pretraining and extension. Additionally, it serves as an empirical baseline (c) for the performance characteristics of VLA controllers (specifically pi-zero) that a follow-up study on extending such models would need to reference.
